title: Calibrachoa scabridula
intro: Calibrachoa scabridula, also known as the hairy petunia, is a perennial plant species native to South America, characterized by its small, white to pink flowers and hairy stems. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Calibrachoa scabridula include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to provide relief from symptoms associated with various health conditions.
taxonomy: Calibrachoa scabridula is a species of flowering plant in the family Solanaceae. It is native to South America and is often found in dry to wet regions. This species is a member of the Calibrachoa genus, which consists of annual and perennial plants commonly known as million bells or petunia-surrogates. Calibrachoa scabridula is further classified as belonging to the order Solanales and the class Magnoliopsida. It is known for its small, trumpet-shaped flowers that come in a variety of colors.
